evidence.description: Weight Total now sums to 1.00 (FIND-035 resolved); contract uses `bun run test:dom` consistently (FIND-036 resolved at the script-name level); rgba grep uses `0px 23px 52px` substring (FIND-041 resolved).
Property test files exist at the cited paths (FIND-037 resolved).
DOM vitest files live at the cited dom/ subdirectory (FIND-038 resolved).
EC-EDIT regex `0(0[1-9]|1[0-4])` covers exactly the 14 spec IDs (FIND-045 resolved); editor-scope type-check via `bun run check | grep src/lib/editor/` (FIND-044 resolved).

summary: Contract iter-2 fixes 9 of the 13 iter-1 findings cleanly (FIND-035, 036, 037, 038, 040, 041, 044, 045, 047 resolved). However, three substantive iter-1 issues persist (FIND-039 partially, FIND-042 not at all, FIND-043 partially) and four new issues are introduced. Test-runner mismatch is the dominant new defect: `bun run test:dom` runs only `*.dom.vitest.ts` per vitest.config.ts include pattern, but CRIT-703/704/705/714 require pure-tier prop tests and bun:test unit tests to pass via `bun run test:dom`; those tests use `import from 'bun:test'` and are not picked up by vitest. Additional defects: CRIT-702 `kind:` grep still over-counts because three `_Assert*` type aliases in types.ts use `{ kind: 'X' }` syntax (would output 20, not 17); coverage JSON path still queries non-existent `branchCoverage.pct` (actual key is `branches.pct`); SaveFailureBanner contains `#dd5b00` twice (data-accent-color attribute and border-left CSS) so `grep -c == 1` is unsatisfiable; assertion strings use Unicode `≥`/`→` in source but ASCII `>=`/`->` in contract (CRIT-703/704/705/714 cited names mismatch). Phase 3 cannot proceed against this contract: at least four passThresholds remain unevaluable as written and test-runner architecture has not been verified by the builder.
